reasoning: The full taint path from $_POST['template_vars']['query_args']['s'] to the search engine sink was traced. UsCore.php lines 35-43 extract $search_term from attacker-controlled JSON without any sanitization at the call site, and pass it to Helpers::searchProducts($search_term) (Helpers.php line 1655). That function immediately calls DGWT_WCAS()->nativeSearch->getSearchResults($phrase, true, 'product-ids') (Helpers.php line 1660). In WordPressNative/Search.php::getSearchResults(), the $return parameter is true, so the code always takes the branch at line 174-175: $keyword = sanitize_text_field($phrase). The else branch (lines 177-185) is only reached when $return === false, which never happens via this call chain. The sanitization is therefore guaranteed and not conditional. No unsanitized attacker value reaches any dangerous sink (SQL, output, filesystem) in the current code. The reachability claim (nopriv) is accurate — wp_ajax_nopriv_us_ajax_grid is registered with no capability or nonce check, and isRelevantProductAjaxQuery() only gates on post_type being 'product'. However, the impact is nil because the sanitization executes on every invocation. The finding describes a defense-in-depth gap (reliance on downstream sanitization rather than sanitizing at the entry point), not an exploitable vulnerability. Marked false positive.
